---
title: Kitchen Sink Test Runbook
---

# Kitchen Sink Test Runbook

This runbook exercises every feature of Runbooks to validate the Electron rewrite. Walk through each section and confirm everything renders and functions correctly.

---

## 1. Markdown Rendering

### Text Formatting

**Bold text**, *italic text*, ***bold italic***, ~~strikethrough~~, and `inline code`.

### Lists

**Unordered:**
- Item 1
- Item 2
  - Nested 2.1
  - Nested 2.2
- Item 3

**Ordered:**
1. First
2. Second
   1. Nested numbered
   2. Another nested
3. Third

### Code Blocks

```go
func main() {
    fmt.Println("Hello from Go!")
}
```

```python
def hello():
    print("Hello from Python!")
```

```bash
echo "Hello from Bash!"
```

### Tables

| Feature | Status | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| Markdown | Working | Full GFM support |
| Blocks | Working | All types |
| Templates | Working | Boilerplate engine |

### Blockquotes

> This is a blockquote.
>
> > Nested blockquote!

### Task Lists

- [x] Completed task
- [ ] Incomplete task

### Links

[Runbooks Documentation](https://runbooks.gruntwork.io)

---

## 2. Admonitions

<Admonition type="note" title="Note" description="This is a **note** admonition with _inline markdown_." />

<Admonition type="info" title="Info" description="This is an **info** admonition." />

<Admonition type="warning" title="Warning" description="This is a **warning** admonition." closable={true} />

<Admonition type="danger" title="Danger" description="This is a **danger** admonition. Requires confirmation." confirmationText="I understand the risks" />

---

## 3. All Variable Types & Validations

This Inputs block exercises every variable type and validation rule.

<Inputs id="all-types">
```yaml
variables:
  - name: plain_string
    type: string
    description: A plain string with a default
    default: "hello world"

  - name: required_string
    type: string
    description: This field is required
    validations:
      - required

  - name: email_field
    type: string
    description: Must be a valid email address
    default: "user@example.com"
    validations:
      - email

  - name: url_field
    type: string
    description: Must be a valid URL
    default: "https://example.com"
    validations:
      - url

  - name: regex_field
    type: string
    description: "Must match ^[A-Z]{3}$ (three uppercase letters)"
    default: "ABC"
    validations:
      - type: regex
        regex: "^[A-Z]{3}$"

  - name: length_field
    type: string
    description: Must be 2-10 characters
    default: "hello"
    validations:
      - type: length
        min: 2
        max: 10

  - name: semver_field
    type: string
    description: Must be a valid semver
    default: "1.2.3"
    validations:
      - semver

  - name: alpha_field
    type: string
    description: Alphabetic characters only
    default: "abcdef"
    validations:
      - alpha

  - name: digit_field
    type: string
    description: Digits only
    default: "12345"
    validations:
      - digit

  - name: alphanumeric_field
    type: string
    description: Alphanumeric characters only
    default: "abc123"
    validations:
      - alphanumeric

  - name: int_field
    type: int
    description: An integer input
    default: 42

  - name: bool_field
    type: bool
    description: A boolean toggle
    default: true

  - name: enum_field
    type: enum
    description: Pick an environment
    options:
      - dev
      - staging
      - prod
    default: dev

  - name: list_field
    type: list
    description: A dynamic list of items

  - name: map_field
    type: map
    description: A key-value map

  - name: secret_field
    type: string
    description: A sensitive password field (should be masked)
    sensitive: true
    default: "s3cret"
```
</Inputs>

---

## 4. Simple Commands

### Inline Command

<Command
  id="simple-inline-cmd"
  command="echo 'Hello from an inline command!'"
  title="Simple Inline Command"
  description="Tests the simplest possible command"
  successMessage="Inline command **succeeded**!"
  failMessage="Inline command failed."
  runningMessage="Running inline command..."
/>

### Command from Script

<Command
  id="setup-outputs"
  path="scripts/setup-outputs.sh"
  title="Setup Block Outputs"
  description="Runs a script that writes key=value pairs to `$RUNBOOK_OUTPUT`"
  successMessage="Outputs produced: account_id, region, project_name"
  failMessage="Script failed."
/>

### Command with inputsId

<Command
  id="cmd-with-inputs"
  command="echo 'String: {{ .inputs.plain_string }}, Int: {{ .inputs.int_field }}, Bool: {{ .inputs.bool_field }}, Enum: {{ .inputs.enum_field }}'"
  inputsId="all-types"
  title="Command Using Inputs: {{ .inputs.enum_field }}"
  description="Reads from the all-types Inputs block. Current env: **{{ .inputs.enum_field }}**"
/>

---

## 5. Block Outputs

The `setup-outputs` command above wrote outputs. Now let's consume them downstream.

### Consuming Outputs in Description

<Command
  id="consume-outputs"
  command="echo 'Working in account {{ .outputs.setup_outputs.account_id }} in {{ .outputs.setup_outputs.region }}'"
  title="Consume Block Outputs"
  description="Account: {{ .outputs.setup_outputs.account_id }}, Region: {{ .outputs.setup_outputs.region }}"
  successMessage="Successfully read outputs from setup-outputs block!"
/>

### Command with Inline Inputs + Outputs

<Command
  id="cmd-inline-inputs"
  path="scripts/mixed-inputs-outputs.sh"
  title="Command with Inline Inputs"
  description="Uses both inline inputs and outputs from `setup-outputs`"
>
  <Inputs id="inline-cmd-vars">
    ```yaml
    variables:
      - name: Greeting
        type: string
        description: A greeting to repeat
        default: "Howdy"
      - name: Count
        type: int
        description: How many times to greet
        default: 3
    ```
  </Inputs>
</Command>

---

## 6. Complex Outputs (JSON Lists & Maps)

<Command
  id="list-complex-data"
  path="scripts/list-complex-data.sh"
  title="Produce Complex Outputs"
  description="Outputs JSON lists and maps via `$RUNBOOK_OUTPUT`"
  successMessage="Complex data produced!"
/>

### Iterating Over a List

<TemplateInline id="users-list" outputPath="users.txt">
```txt
=== Organization Users ===
{{- range (fromJson .outputs.list_complex_data.users) }}
- {{ . }}
{{- end }}
```
</TemplateInline>

### Iterating Over a Map

<TemplateInline id="teams-list" outputPath="teams.yaml">
```yaml
teams:
{{- range $teamName, $teamData := (fromJson .outputs.list_complex_data.teams) }}
  {{ $teamName }}:
    lead: {{ $teamData.lead }}
    members:
    {{- range $teamData.members }}
      - {{ . }}
    {{- end }}
{{- end }}
```
</TemplateInline>

---

## 7. Check Blocks

### Successful Check

<Check
  id="check-pass"
  path="checks/simple-pass.sh"
  title="Check That Passes"
  description="This check exits 0 (success)"
  successMessage="Check **passed** as expected!"
  failMessage="This should not appear."
/>

### Warning Check (Exit Code 2)

<Check
  id="check-warn"
  path="checks/simple-warn.sh"
  title="Check That Warns"
  description="This check exits 2 (warning)"
  successMessage="This should not appear."
  warnMessage="Warning detected! Something needs attention."
  failMessage="This should not appear either."
/>

### Check with Inline Inputs

<Check
  id="check-with-inputs"
  command="echo 'Checking tool version: {{ .inputs.ToolVersion }}' && exit 0"
  title="Parameterized Check"
  description="A check with an inline Inputs form"
  successMessage="Version {{ .inputs.ToolVersion }} looks good!"
  failMessage="Version check failed."
>
  <Inputs id="check-tool-inputs">
    ```yaml
    variables:
      - name: ToolVersion
        type: string
        description: Expected tool version
        default: "1.0.0"
        validations:
          - required
          - semver
    ```
  </Inputs>
</Check>

---

## 8. Template Block

This Template reads from `templates/sample-config/` which has a `boilerplate.yml` with `x-section` grouping. It also references outputs from `setup-outputs`, testing output dependency tracking.

<Template id="sample-config" path="templates/sample-config" />

---

## 9. TemplateInline

### Simple TemplateInline with inputsId

<TemplateInline id="simple-inline-tpl" inputsId="all-types" outputPath="summary.txt">
```txt
Project Summary
===============
String: {{ .inputs.plain_string }}
Environment: {{ .inputs.enum_field }}
Integer: {{ .inputs.int_field }}
Boolean: {{ .inputs.bool_field }}
```
</TemplateInline>

### TemplateInline with Block Outputs

<TemplateInline id="output-preview" outputPath="account-info.txt">
```txt
Account Information
===================
Account ID: {{ .outputs.setup_outputs.account_id }}
Region:     {{ .outputs.setup_outputs.region }}
Project:    {{ .outputs.setup_outputs.project_name }}
```
</TemplateInline>

### TemplateInline with generateFile

<TemplateInline id="gen-file-tpl" inputsId="all-types" outputPath="generated.yaml" generateFile={true}>
```yaml
name: {{ .inputs.plain_string }}
env: {{ .inputs.enum_field }}
port: {{ .inputs.int_field }}
```
</TemplateInline>

### Combining Inputs and Outputs

<TemplateInline id="combined-tpl" inputsId="all-types" outputPath="combined.json">
```json
{
  "project": "{{ .inputs.plain_string }}",
  "environment": "{{ .inputs.enum_field }}",
  "account_id": "{{ .outputs.setup_outputs.account_id }}",
  "region": "{{ .outputs.setup_outputs.region }}"
}
```
</TemplateInline>

---

## 10. Environment & Working Directory Persistence

### Set Environment Variables

<Command
  id="set-env"
  path="scripts/use-env-persistence.sh"
  title="Set Environment Variables"
  description="Exports `KS_ENV_VAR` and `KS_COUNTER`. These should persist to subsequent blocks."
  successMessage="Environment variables exported!"
/>

### Verify Environment Persistence

<Check
  id="verify-env"
  path="checks/verify-env.sh"
  title="Verify Env Vars Persisted"
  description="Checks that `KS_ENV_VAR` and `KS_COUNTER` from the previous block are available."
  successMessage="Environment variables persisted!"
  failMessage="Environment variables did NOT persist. Run the block above first."
/>

### Change Working Directory

<Command
  id="change-dir"
  path="scripts/change-workdir.sh"
  title="Change Working Directory"
  description="Changes the working directory to `/tmp`."
  successMessage="Working directory changed to /tmp!"
/>

### Verify Working Directory Persistence

<Check
  id="verify-workdir"
  path="checks/verify-workdir.sh"
  title="Verify Working Directory"
  description="Confirms the working directory is still `/tmp`."
  successMessage="Working directory persisted!"
  failMessage="Working directory did not persist."
/>

---

## 11. File Capture ($GENERATED_FILES)

<Command
  id="capture-files"
  path="scripts/generate-files.sh"
  title="Generate and Capture Files"
  description="Writes files to `$GENERATED_FILES`. Check the Generated Files panel after running."
  successMessage="Files generated! Check the Generated Files panel."
  failMessage="File generation failed."
/>

---

## 12. Script Logging

<Command
  id="logging-demo"
  path="scripts/logging-demo.sh"
  title="Script Logging Demo"
  description="Exercises `log_info`, `log_warn`, `log_error`, and `log_debug`"
  successMessage="All log levels exercised!"
/>

---

## 13. Multiple inputsId (Merging)

When `inputsId` is an array, variables from later Inputs blocks override earlier ones.

<Inputs id="defaults-merge">
```yaml
variables:
  - name: color
    type: string
    description: Favorite color
    default: "blue"
  - name: size
    type: string
    description: T-shirt size
    default: "medium"
```
</Inputs>

<Inputs id="overrides-merge">
```yaml
variables:
  - name: size
    type: string
    description: "T-shirt size (this override should win)"
    default: "large"
```
</Inputs>

<Command
  id="merged-inputs-cmd"
  inputsId={["defaults-merge", "overrides-merge"]}
  command="echo 'Color: {{ .inputs.color }}, Size: {{ .inputs.size }}'"
  title="Merged Inputs"
  description="Uses `inputsId` as an array. Color comes from first, size should show the override value."
/>

---

## 14. Auth Blocks

These render the authentication UI. Skip if you don't have credentials -- the blocks below use mock commands.

<AwsAuth
  id="aws-auth-test"
  title="AWS Authentication (Optional)"
  description="Tests AwsAuth block rendering."
  defaultRegion="us-west-2"
  detectCredentials={false}
/>

<GitHubAuth
  id="gh-auth-test"
  title="GitHub Authentication (Optional)"
  description="Tests GitHubAuth block rendering."
/>

### Command with awsAuthId

<Command
  id="aws-cmd"
  command="echo 'AWS auth context would be active here'"
  title="AWS-Authenticated Command"
  description="This command is gated on AWS authentication."
  awsAuthId="aws-auth-test"
  successMessage="AWS command ran!"
/>

### Command with githubAuthId

<Command
  id="gh-cmd"
  command="echo 'GitHub auth context would be active here'"
  title="GitHub-Authenticated Command"
  description="This command is gated on GitHub authentication."
  githubAuthId="gh-auth-test"
  successMessage="GitHub command ran!"
/>

---

## 15. GitClone

<GitClone
  id="clone-test"
  title="Clone a Repository"
  description="Tests the GitClone block. Enter a public repo URL to clone."
  prefilledUrl="https://github.com/gruntwork-io/runbooks.git"
  prefilledRef="main"
  showFileTree={true}
/>

---

## 16. GitHubPullRequest

<GitHubPullRequest
  id="pr-test"
  githubAuthId="gh-auth-test"
  title="Create a Pull Request"
  description="Tests the GitHubPullRequest block rendering."
  prefilledPullRequestTitle="Test PR from Kitchen Sink"
  prefilledPullRequestDescription="## Summary\n- Generated by the kitchen sink test runbook"
  prefilledPullRequestLabels={["test", "kitchen-sink"]}
  prefilledBranchName="test/kitchen-sink"
/>

---

## 17. DirPicker

<DirPicker
  id="dir-picker-test"
  rootDir="."
  title="Pick a Directory"
  description="Tests the DirPicker block with labels."
  pathLabel="Target Path"
  pathLabelDescription="Choose a directory to inspect"
  dirLabels={["Category", "Subcategory"]}
/>

---

## 18. Template Expressions in Block Props

This section confirms that `{{ .inputs.* }}` and `{{ .outputs.* }}` resolve correctly in `title` and `description` props across various block types.

<Command
  id="expr-test"
  command="echo 'Expressions resolved successfully'"
  inputsId="all-types"
  title="Deploy to {{ .inputs.enum_field }}"
  description="Project **{{ .inputs.plain_string }}** deploying to account {{ .outputs.setup_outputs.account_id }} in {{ .outputs.setup_outputs.region }}"
  successMessage="Expressions in props work!"
/>

<Check
  id="expr-check"
  command="exit 0"
  inputsId="all-types"
  title="Validate {{ .inputs.enum_field }} environment"
  description="Checking account {{ .outputs.setup_outputs.account_id }}"
  successMessage="Expression check passed!"
/>

---

## Done!

If every section above rendered correctly and all Commands/Checks ran as expected, the Electron rewrite is in good shape.
